attributionCleanup.method: git filter-branch --msg-filter over the two affected commits, preserving author and committer identity and dates

defectsFound: a target file that is itself a symlink resolving outside the controlled root passed classification and would have been written through (fixed: canonical full-path escape check added to the target policy)
target writes used plain fs::write, so a crash mid-write could truncate the target (fixed: all target writes and restores now go through atomic temp-file-plus-rename)

activeConfigPilot.rehearsalStatus: proven against the real active config content in a temp copy; source untouched

gui.added: review-only, report-backed 'Structured-family write status' card on the Config page with a permanently insensitive Apply control labeled as blocked pending pilot approval
gui.gtkEvidenceResult: accessibility inspection succeeded; the new card title and blocked Apply control label are proven via AT-SPI text; all safety flags (applyClicked, hyprlandReloaded, realConfigEdited, runtimeMutated, mutatingHyprctlUsed) are false

runtimeReloadIntegration: unchanged: no reload path exists, no reload was run, no runtime mutation occurred; the pilot models the reload/runtime boundary as an evidence-backed gate
hyprland0554Migration: unchanged: advisory only, blocked by missing trusted official export data
packagingRelease: unchanged: v0.1.0 tag and dist/v0.1.0 artifacts preserved; no new tag or release created
productionReadinessDecision: not production ready for active real config writes
nextRecommendedWork: Stop for explicit user decision: approve or reject a first active real config write pilot for structured families. Unblocking it requires either disabling compositor config autoreload (misc:disable_autoreload=true) or explicitly approving the runtime reload that the write would trigger.
